remote_jobs.title: Agentic AI Developer
remote_jobs.summary: ManTech is hiring: Agentic AI Developer. Fully remote (USA) full-time role building agentic AI systems for enterprise and government programs; no security clearance required (Req R68149).

remote_jobs.title: Localization Web Developer
remote_jobs.summary: NinjaOne is hiring: Localization Web Developer. Fully remote role owning localization of NinjaOne's web properties — web development plus internationalization tooling and workflows.

remote_jobs.title: Managing Counsel, Commercial - Partnerships
remote_jobs.summary: Remote is hiring: Managing Counsel, Commercial - Partnerships. Remote (the global-employment platform) seeks a commercial counsel for partnerships — senior legal role on a fully distributed team.

remote_jobs.title: Help Desk Support Services Specialist
remote_jobs.summary: ManTech is hiring: Help Desk Support Services Specialist. Fully remote (USA) help-desk support role; requires an active Secret clearance (Req R69112).

remote_jobs.title: Data Engineer, Engineering & Operations
remote_jobs.summary: NBCUniversal is hiring: Data Engineer, Engineering & Operations. Remote data-engineering role in NBCUniversal's Engineering & Operations group supporting its film, TV and streaming platforms.

remote_jobs.title: Adjunct Faculty, Information Sciences and Technology
remote_jobs.summary: George Mason University is hiring: Adjunct Faculty, Information Sciences and Technology. Part-time adjunct teaching in Information Sciences and Technology; on-site/hybrid options across George Mason's Virginia campuses with some remote sections.

remote_jobs.title: Data Integrator
remote_jobs.summary: ManTech is hiring: Data Integrator. Fully remote (USA, FL-based team) data-integration role; requires an active Secret clearance (Req R68506).

remote_jobs.title: Science Adjunct Instructor
remote_jobs.summary: California Institute of Applied Technology is hiring: Science Adjunct Instructor. Part-time evening science instruction, work-from-home; must reside in CA, FL, MA or NM.

ai_search_trends.title: Claude's Criminally Bad Electron Mac App Is an Inside Job
Agentic coding notes from Galapagos Island
New serious vulnerabilities spiked around release of Claude Mythos Preview
Coding without AI: a revolutionary new way to work
Kagi Changelog (July 2): Heads, tails, and an AI toggle
AI First: How the Federal Government Is Prioritizing AI over People and Planet
Meta AI chief says their coming LLM has caught up with OpenAI's flagship model
Save Claude Code Tokens with Smart Routing
CueBench for Developers is live: score how well you drive coding agents

anthropic_claude_news.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
anthropic_claude_news.summary: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software and barred employees from using it, citing alleged backdoor concerns — mirroring earlier bans flowing the other way across the US-China AI divide. It is one of the clearest signs yet that coding agents are now treated as strategic infrastructure. Automation angle: Geopolitical tool bans fragment the agent-tooling market — position your portfolio around open, self-hostable automation stacks (MCP, n8n) that survive vendor bans.

anthropic_claude_news.title: Anthropic developer shares prompting tips for Fable 5 that focus on finding your own blind spots first
anthropic_claude_news.summary: Anthropic developer Thariq Shihipar argues that with Fable 5 the bottleneck is no longer the model but the user's own blind spots. He recommends techniques like blind-spot prompting — asking the model to surface what you haven't considered — before dictating a plan. Automation angle: Turn 'blind-spot passes' into a reusable Claude Code slash command in your portfolio — it's a cheap, demonstrable quality gate for any agent workflow.

anthropic_claude_news.title: Anthropic launches its own drug discovery programs to tackle diseases Big Pharma considers unprofitable
anthropic_claude_news.summary: Anthropic is launching its own drug-development programs targeting neglected diseases that Big Pharma considers unprofitable, moving beyond selling models into running AI-native science itself. Novartis's CEO has separately said AI could cut drug development time dramatically. Automation angle: Watch for Claude Science APIs — vertical 'AI-runs-the-workflow' plays like this are the template for automation businesses in every unglamorous domain.

ai_business_automation.title: Major Union Livid After 1,000 Factory Workers Were Replaced With 50 Robots
ai_business_automation.summary: The UAW is livid after General Motors replaced 1,000 factory workers with just 50 AI-guided robots, a 20-to-1 substitution the union calls 'taking away jobs from people.' It's one of the starkest single-site datapoints yet on physical automation economics. Automation angle: The ROI math that justifies 50-robots-for-1,000-workers is spreading to back-office work — learn to build the business case spreadsheet, not just the automation.

quantum_ai_research.title: Researchers Propose Thermodynamic Computing Architecture That Could Dramatically Reduce AI Energy Use
quantum_ai_research.summary: Researchers propose a thermodynamic computing architecture that exploits physical noise as a computational resource, potentially cutting the energy cost of AI workloads by orders of magnitude versus digital accelerators. It targets exactly the sampling-heavy operations that dominate generative model inference. Automation angle: Energy is the looming cost wall for AI automation at scale — track unconventional-compute vendors now; cheap inference changes which workflows are economical to automate.

quantum_ai_research.title: South Korea’s Deputy PM: “After AI Comes Quantum” — Unveils Blueprint for approximately $644.8 Billion Investment Linking Industry, Academia, and Research - finance.biggo.com
quantum_ai_research.summary: South Korea's Deputy PM unveiled a roughly $644.8 billion blueprint linking industry, academia and research under the slogan 'After AI Comes Quantum,' explicitly treating quantum as the next national platform after its AI buildout. Automation angle: Government mega-programs seed grants and pilot procurement — watch for Korea-linked quantum-AI startup programs open to foreign builders.

quantum_ai_research.title: Samsung Chases TSMC With Quantum Computing-Powered Chipmaking as AI Reshapes the Most Critical Step in Fabrication - Wccftech
quantum_ai_research.summary: Samsung is reportedly applying quantum computing to optical proximity correction — the most computationally brutal step in chip fabrication — to chase TSMC, as AI demand reshapes fab economics. Quantum-assisted lithography would directly accelerate the very chips AI runs on. Automation angle: A quantum-AI-semiconductor flywheel is forming; expect cheaper accelerators downstream — plan automation offerings that assume falling inference cost.

quantum_ai_research.title: XRP engineer warns quantum computing threat to crypto could arrive by 2028, accelerated by AI advances. - Pluang
quantum_ai_research.summary: An XRP engineer warns that the quantum threat to crypto could arrive as early as 2028, with AI advances accelerating both quantum hardware design and cryptanalysis research. The estimate compresses earlier 2030s timelines. Automation angle: Post-quantum migration is a coming compliance wave — an automation service that inventories a company's crypto dependencies is a startable, dateable business.

quantum_ai_research.title: CIA reorganizes to embrace AI and quantum computing - SC Media
quantum_ai_research.summary: The CIA is reorganizing to put AI and quantum computing at the center of its tradecraft, standing up structures to adopt both technologies faster. Intelligence agencies openly treating quantum-plus-AI as one combined capability is itself notable. Automation angle: Government adoption drags a long vendor tail — cleared-sector AI automation and quantum-readiness consulting are growing hiring niches.

quantum_ai_research.title: The Emerging Era of Quantum-Native Agents | by Mohit Sewak, Ph.D. | Jul, 2026 - DataDrivenInvestor
quantum_ai_research.summary: A DataDrivenInvestor deep-dive maps the emerging idea of quantum-native agents: AI agents whose planning or sampling subroutines run on quantum hardware rather than merely calling classical LLMs. It sketches near-term hybrid architectures where agents dispatch optimization steps to QPUs. Automation angle: Agent frameworks will grow 'quantum tool' plugins — the same MCP-style tool-routing skills you're building apply directly when QPU endpoints appear.

quantum_ai_research.title: QFedAgent: Quantum-Enhanced Personalized Federated Learning for Multi-Agent Activity Recognition
quantum_ai_research.summary: QFedAgent proposes quantum-enhanced personalized federated learning for multi-agent activity recognition, using quantum circuits to fuse heterogeneous sensor streams that degrade classical federated learning. It's a concrete quantum-ML answer to non-IID multi-agent data. Automation angle: Privacy-preserving multi-agent learning is a real enterprise pain point — even the classical half of this design is a sellable pattern for sensor-heavy clients.

quantum_ai_research.title: Neural-Network Inverse Design of SRF Cavities and Transmons for Bosonic Quantum Computation
quantum_ai_research.summary: Researchers use neural networks to inverse-design superconducting RF cavities coupled to transmon qubits for bosonic quantum computing — recovering device geometries from target electromagnetic specs. It's machine learning directly accelerating quantum-hardware engineering. Automation angle: ML-for-hardware-design is spreading from chips to qubits — 'AI-assisted engineering design' automations are transferable across every physical-product industry.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Global AI Hackathon Series with Qwen Cloud
AMD Developer Hackathon: ACT II
product_showcase_opportunities.summary:
To participate in the hackathon, simply click Sign up with AMD. If you are not already a member of the AMD AI Developer Program (ADP), you will need to create an account.

ADP members can access AI tutorials, experts, and community support. In addition, new members can claim a free month of DeepLearning.ai pro, $100 in AMD GPU cloud credits, and $50 in Fireworks AI API credits. 

All hackathon participants will receive on hackathon launch additional compute and API credits for their projects.
Re

IndiaAI Startups Global - International Acceleration Programs for Indian AI Startups
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: Placement into international acceleration tracks, plus government backing and global market access.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: MeitY / IndiaAI international acceleration tracks placing Indian AI startups into global programs. Rolling cohorts.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Y Combinator (AI startups)
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Any early-stage startup with a founding team; pre-launch and idea-stage are welcome.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: ~$500K on the standard deal, a 3-month program, and the lifelong YC network + Demo Day.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Antler Residency (AI)
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Solo founders welcome — no company or co-founder needed.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: Pre-seed cash, a co-founder matching residency, and hands-on mentorship.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Day-zero residency for founders incl. AI; rolling cohorts across 30+ cities.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Techstars AI Accelerators
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Early-stage startups; apply to the city/track that fits your team.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: Seed investment, a 3-month mentor-driven program, and a global investor network.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Multiple AI-focused Techstars programs; rolling applications by location.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: NVIDIA Inception
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Any AI/ML startup at any stage — free to join.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: GPU and cloud credits, go-to-market support, and introductions to VCs.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Free program for AI/ML startups: GPU credits, go-to-market, VC intros. Rolling.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Google for Startups AI Accelerator
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Seed-to-Series-A AI startups; regional tracks including India.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: An equity-free program, Google mentorship, and cloud credits.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Equity-free AI accelerator with seasonal cohorts and regional tracks (incl. India).

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Inviting AI-first startups to apply for Google for Startups Accelerator: India - blog.google
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Inviting AI-first startups to apply for Google for Startups Accelerator: India - blog.google

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Apply Now: Google Accelerator Support for AI Startups in India - ICTworks
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Apply Now: Google Accelerator Support for AI Startups in India - ICTworks

unaddressed_ai_problems.title: A 26,000-student study shows AI's hidden learning cost takes two full years to surface
unaddressed_ai_problems.summary: A 26,000-student Chinese study found AI users finished homework faster and scored better — then performed up to 24 percent worse on exams, with the damage taking two full years to surface. Nobody currently ships tooling that measures long-horizon learning loss, so schools are flying blind. Automation angle: 'AI that teaches without deskilling' is a wide-open product category — retention-aware tutoring workflows would differentiate instantly.

unaddressed_ai_problems.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
unaddressed_ai_problems.summary: Fan-writing communities are purging suspected AI-written fiction using detection methods that are known to false-positive, catching human authors in the crossfire. Reliable AI-text attribution remains unsolved, yet communities, schools and courts keep acting as if it exists. Automation angle: Detection is a dead end — provenance is the gap: cryptographic 'proof-of-human-process' writing tools are an unbuilt, urgently wanted product.

global_ai_news.title: Meta Paid Hundreds of Contractors to Pretend to Be Teenagers While Barraging Its Competitors’ AI With Disturbing Content
global_ai_news.summary: Meta reportedly paid hundreds of contractors to pose as teenagers and barrage rival AI chatbots with disturbing prompts about suicide, sex and drugs — apparently to probe competitors' safety behavior. Leaked chats show contractors themselves asking whether they'd get in trouble. Automation angle: Adversarial 'teen persona' probing is now industrialized — if you ship a public chatbot, red-team it for exactly this class of prompt before someone else does.

global_ai_news.title: OpenAI cofounder envisions "almost no interface" future where nobody learns software anymore
global_ai_news.summary: OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman says ChatGPT plugins failed because the models weren't ready, and envisions an 'almost no interface' future where a context-aware agent quietly does the work and nobody learns software UIs anymore. Automation angle: If interfaces dissolve into agents, the sellable skill is workflow design — practice specifying outcomes, guardrails and handoffs rather than clicking through tools.

global_ai_news.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
global_ai_news.summary: TechCrunch profiles Mistral AI, the French open-weights champion that has raised billions since 2023 on the promise of putting frontier AI in everyone's hands. The explainer lands as Mistral's releases increasingly anchor Europe's answer to OpenAI and Anthropic. Automation angle: Open-weights models keep automation costs predictable — keep a Mistral fallback path in client workflows to hedge API pricing and vendor lock-in.

global_ai_news.title: Surprise! Meta Says Now You Have to Pay a Monthly Subscription to Use Key Features of Your Already Expensive Smart Glasses
global_ai_news.summary: Meta now charges a monthly subscription to unlock key AI features on its already-expensive smart glasses, moving core assistant capabilities behind a paywall after purchase. Buyers are calling the move 'utterly bogus.' Automation angle: Hardware-plus-AI-subscription is becoming the default consumer model — price your automation offers as recurring service, not one-off builds.

indian_ai_industry.title: Indian IT firms likely to report muted Q1 growth amid AI spending, macro uncertainty
indian_ai_industry.summary: India's IT majors are expected to post another muted quarter as enterprises redirect tech budgets toward AI and hold back conventional spending amid macro uncertainty. Addressable spend for the big services firms keeps softening year-on-year. Automation angle: Budgets are leaving legacy IT services for AI work — exactly the arbitrage a solo AI-automation builder can sell into Indian SMBs right now.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Don’t Be Afraid of Self-Improving AI, Says a16z-Backed Startup Mirendil - Gizmodo
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: a16z-backed startup Mirendil, fresh off a $200 million raise, argues self-improving AI shouldn't be feared and is building a platform where models continuously retrain and upgrade themselves in production. It's the most direct commercial bet yet on recursive self-improvement as a product. Automation angle: Self-updating models break today's eval-then-deploy pipelines — continuous-evaluation harnesses for self-improving AI systems are an open tooling gap to build in.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Recursive Self-Improvement Now Has a Co-Evolving Evaluator: Cambridge-NVIDIA Paper Raises the Stakes - Tech Times
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: A Cambridge-NVIDIA paper pairs a self-improving AI model with a co-evolving evaluator, so the judge of improvements gets stronger alongside the system it scores — attacking the stale-evaluator failure mode that stalls recursive self-improvement loops. Automation angle: The co-evolving-judge pattern is directly portable: pair every agent you ship with an eval agent that upgrades whenever the worker does.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Ornith-1.0: Self-Scaffolding LLMs for Agentic Coding
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: DeepReinforce released Ornith-1.0, MIT-licensed 'self-scaffolding' LLMs (9B to 397B, built on Gemma 4 and Qwen 3.5) that construct and refine their own agentic coding scaffolds instead of relying on hand-written harnesses. Simon Willison flags it as a notable open-weights first. Automation angle: Try Ornith's self-scaffolding loop against your Claude Code workflows — models that write their own harnesses hint at where agent frameworks are heading.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: EvoPolicyGym: Evaluating Autonomous Policy Evolution in Interactive Environments
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: EvoPolicyGym introduces a controlled benchmark for autonomous policy evolution: an agent repeatedly edits its own executable policy under a fixed interaction budget, so self-improvement can finally be measured instead of inferred from a single end score. Automation angle: Benchmark your own agents the same way — measure improvement-per-iteration, not just final accuracy, when you pitch automation results to clients.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Neuron-Aware Data Selection for Annotation-Free LLM Self-Distillation
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: This arXiv work selects self-distillation training data by watching which neurons activate, letting an LLM improve itself in specialist domains without any human labels or interaction feedback. It sharpens the emerging recipe of models supervising their own post-training. Automation angle: Label-free self-improvement slashes fine-tuning cost — flag this to any client who says custom models are too expensive to maintain.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: DemoPSD: Disagreement-Modulated Policy Self-Distillation
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: DemoPSD refines on-policy self-distillation — where one AI model teaches itself using privileged information as its own teacher — by modulating supervision with teacher-student disagreement to stop the self-training loop overfitting in-domain. Automation angle: Self-distillation is becoming the default cheap upgrade path for reasoning models — worth a portfolio experiment on a small open model.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Text / LLM - Best: Claude Fable 5 / Runner-up: GPT-5.x
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Anthropic's new Claude 5 family (Fable 5, with Sonnet 5 rolling out) tops general assistant quality this week; GPT-5.x holds runner-up. Automation angle: Switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Coding - Best: Claude Fable 5 (via Claude Code) / Runner-up: GPT-5 Codex
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Fable 5 set a new AI freelance-work performance record this week and leads agentic coding via Claude Code; Grok 4.5's claimed parity remains unbenchmarked in private beta. Automation angle: Switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Image Generation - Best: ChatGPT Images 2.0 / Runner-up: Google Nano Banana 2
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: ChatGPT Images 2.0 keeps the top spot; Google's Nano Banana 2 line moves into runner-up on quality-per-dollar. Automation angle: Switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Video Generation - Best: Sora 2 / Runner-up: Google Veo 3
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Sora 2 retains the video crown with Veo 3 close behind; no ranking-moving release this week. Automation angle: Switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Music / Audio - Best: Suno v5 / Runner-up: Udio v2.5
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Suno v5 still leads music generation as Suno explores a developer API program; Udio v2.5 remains runner-up. Automation angle: Switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Reasoning / Math - Best: GPT-5.x (extended thinking) / Runner-up: Claude Fable 5
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: GPT-5.x extended-thinking holds the math/reasoning lead; Fable 5 is runner-up, while Mistral's Leanstral 1.5 now owns the formal-proof niche. Automation angle: Switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Mistral's open-source Leanstral 1.5 aces formal math benchmarks and catches real bugs in code
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Mistral released Leanstral 1.5, an open-source model for formal verification in Lean 4 that aces formal-math benchmarks — and found five previously unknown bugs while scanning 57 open-source repositories. Formal methods plus LLMs are quietly becoming a practical bug-hunting stack. Automation angle: Formally-verified code review is a fresh service niche — pair Leanstral-style checkers with your coding-agent workflows for high-trust clients.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: UK's AI Security Institute finds standard benchmarks systematically underestimate what AI agents can actually do
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: The UK AI Security Institute showed across seven benchmarks that standard evals systematically underestimate AI agents because they cap compute budgets; given more attempts and thinking time, agents clear far more tasks. Published capability numbers are floors, not ceilings. Automation angle: When scoping automation feasibility, test agents at realistic retry budgets — benchmark scores understate what your production loop can do.

general_news.title: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
general_news.summary: Huge crowds gathered in Tehran on the first day of former supreme leader Ali Khamenei's funeral, with his body lying in state at the Grand Mosalla before processing across Iran and Iraq. Regional figures including Hezbollah and Hamas members attended alongside Iran's leadership.

general_news.title: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
general_news.summary: Ukraine struck a major oil terminal in St Petersburg, with President Zelensky saying the target is key infrastructure funding Russia's war. Moscow's defence ministry vowed the attacks 'will not go unanswered.'

general_news.title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
general_news.summary: Keiko Fujimori was declared winner of Peru's presidential election nearly a month after the vote, capping a razor-thin race. The daughter of disgraced ex-president Alberto Fujimori completes a remarkable political comeback.

general_news.title: US Celebrates 250th Birthday As Trump Says American Identity "Under Attack"
general_news.summary: Millions of Americans marked the country's 250th birthday, as President Trump used the anniversary to claim American identity is 'under attack.' Celebrations were scaled back in several cities by a brutal heatwave.

general_news.title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
general_news.summary: Dangerous heat and humidity blanketing more than 165 million Americans forced cancellation of Fourth of July events from Washington DC to Philadelphia, with temperatures stuck near 38°C for days.

general_news.title: Super Typhoon Bavi Approaches US Pacific Islands, Could Strike Monday
general_news.summary: Super Typhoon Bavi is bearing down on US Pacific islands and could strike Monday, with the storm's eye forecast to pass close to inhabited territories at dangerous intensity.

general_news.title: "Deeply Disturbing": Germany On Reports Of China Training Russian Troops
general_news.summary: Germany called reports that China is training Russian troops 'deeply disturbing,' sharpening European concern over Beijing's support for Moscow's war effort.

general_news.title: Dior dress, Adam Sandler and a man of honour: What we know about Taylor and Travis's wedding
general_news.summary: Taylor Swift married Travis Kelce in a New York ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler, ending months of speculation — details from the Dior dress to the 'man of honour' dominated global feeds.
